label: Performance
summary: Repeated work, allocation churn, and avoidable hot-path waste.

rules.label: Rust Aos Hot Path
rules.description: Repeated struct-field dereferences inside a loop that may indicate an array-of-structs hot path.
rules.explanation: In Rust, Rust Aos Hot Path matters because the pattern here, Repeated struct-field dereferences inside a loop that may indicate an array-of-structs hot path, turns local work into repeated cost that spreads with traffic or input size.
rules.fix: For Rust Aos Hot Path in Rust, move one-time initialization, parsing, or allocation outside the loop when the per-iteration state does not actually change.

rules.label: Rust Blocking Drop
rules.description: A Drop implementation performs blocking work.
rules.explanation: In Rust, Rust Blocking Drop matters because the pattern here, A Drop implementation performs blocking work, is worth review because efficient code is often the result of avoiding small repeated costs before they become default structure.
rules.fix: For Rust Blocking Drop in Rust, reuse allocations, hoist invariant work, and keep the hot path doing only the work that truly belongs there.

rules.label: Rust Blocking I/O in Async
rules.description: Blocking I/O or blocking work observed in async Rust code.
rules.explanation: In Rust, Rust Blocking I/O in Async matters because the pattern here, Blocking I/O or blocking work observed in async Rust code, creates lifecycle behavior that is easy to miss until the system is under load or shutting down.
rules.fix: For Rust Blocking I/O in Async in Rust, give the work an explicit owner, pass the right context or handle, and make cancellation or shutdown visible in the API.
